market_scope.resolution_source: Station-linked source / Weather Underground style whole-degree daily high

ensemble.coverage_note: Ensemble is only available on a short trailing window and should be used for weak confirmation only.

detection.rule: Default regime when the day is neither clearly free-heating nor clearly capped by cloud, rain or fog.

detection.note: Very low sample count in this window; use this regime conservatively.

adjustments.name: gfs_warmer_than_ecmwf
adjustments.reason: GFS can add a small upside check, but ECMWF remains the base for London.

adjustments.name: clear_regime
adjustments.reason: Clear London days usually still add modest upside.

adjustments.name: cloudy_regime
adjustments.reason: Cloudy London days cap upside earlier than Paris.

adjustments.name: rainy_regime
adjustments.reason: Observed rainy London days in this sample had very limited further heating.

adjustments.name: foggy_regime
adjustments.reason: Morning fog can burn off and produce strong delayed heating.

adjustments.name: obs10_above_ecmwf10
adjustments.reason: Observed early warmth versus ECMWF usually implies upside.

adjustments.name: obs10_below_ecmwf10
adjustments.reason: Observed lag versus ECMWF reduces expected high.

adjustments.name: noon_underperformance
adjustments.reason: By noon, London usually cannot recover a large deficit.

adjustments.name: after_14_no_new_high_and_cooling
adjustments.reason: After the main London peak window, cooling should decay higher buckets.

intraday_path_rules.max_by_noon_rule: In most London days, only 0-1C remains after noon; 2C is possible; 3C is a real tail.

fog_burnoff_rule.condition: morning fog clears by late morning and temperatures accelerate into noon
fog_burnoff_rule.effect: Do not keep the day in a suppressed regime; allow delayed upside.

rain_after_high_rule.effect: Lock current high as primary exact bucket and sharply reduce higher bucket probabilities.

sticky_high_rule.effect: Increase confidence that the current high is the final high.

no_new_high_after_14utc_rule.condition: time >= 14 UTC and no new high for at least one hour
no_new_high_after_14utc_rule.effect: Discount the next higher bucket aggressively.

trading_rules.post_14utc_market_scope: Only trade the current observed high bucket and its nearest neighbor.
trading_rules.skip_condition: If absolute edge is below threshold, do not trade.

scenario_invalidation.condition: fresh_METAR moves at least 1C against the scenario

path_break.condition: expected delayed upside fails and cloudy/rain suppression persists

post_peak_confirmation.condition: current_high confirmed by cooling/rain after peak

warnings: Do not use Paris clear-day upside assumptions for London; London's post-noon upside is smaller.
Morning fog at EGLC is a special regime and can produce delayed upside after burn-off.
London often peaks around 13-14 UTC; if no new high appears after 14-15 UTC, the upper tail decays quickly.
Use rainy-regime rules cautiously because the explicit rainy sample in this window is very small.
Ensemble should only be a weak confirmation layer for London in this data set.

operational_summary.primary_city_filter: Morning cloud/fog regime and whether the day accelerates into noon
operational_summary.main_post_noon_rule: London usually has only 0-1C of upside left after noon.
operational_summary.main_post_peak_rule: Once a new high is followed by cooling or rain, treat that high as the most likely final high.
